title: Open Referral UK Schema Manifest
description: Complete metadata and organizational information for all HSDS-UK-3.0 schemas

infrastructure.description: A combined collection of common property definitions for the HSDS profile. Provides reusable property schemas and enum references.
infrastructure.purpose: Centralized definitions and anchors for schema composition
references: All entity schemas reference definitions for properties

infrastructure.description: Centralized collection of enumeration values used across the schema
infrastructure.purpose: Single source of truth for valid enum values

infrastructure.description: Common properties and structure for all entity schemas in HSDS-UK
infrastructure.purpose: Reusable base pattern for entity composition using allOf

infrastructure.description: OpenAPI 3.0.0 specification for the Open Referral UK API
infrastructure.purpose: Defines REST API endpoints, request/response schemas, and documentation

infrastructure.description: Information about this API and its relationship with HSDS
infrastructure.purpose: Provides API metadata and conformance information
Defines pagination structure across all list endpoints

entities.description: The details about each organization delivering services. Each service should be linked to the organization responsible for its delivery. One organization may deliver many services.
The details of the services provided by organizations to a range of different groups.
The other_attributes table creates a link between entities other than service and one or more classifications that describe the entity.
A link between a service and a specific location.
The locations where organizations operate. Locations may be virtual, and one organization may have many locations.
The details of the telephone numbers used to contact organizations, services, and locations.
The details of the named contacts for services and organizations.
The addresses table contains the physical addresses for locations
The details of when a service or location is open. Entries are RFC 5545 RRULES.
The sources of funding for a service or organization.
The details of the geographic area for which a service is available.
The languages that are spoken at locations or services. This does not include languages which can only be used with interpretation.
The details of the arrangements for access to locations for people who have disabilities.
Each service can be categorized according to one or more taxonomy terms. The taxonomy_term object contains a list of taxonomy terms, their names, and, for hierarchical taxonomies, their structure.
The costs of a service at certain points in time. This is an LGA Extension. This object provides a structured version of the text information contained in the 'fees' field of the 'service' object.
The details of the third party identifiers for organizations, such as: company registration number, charity number, tax registration number.
The taxonomies from which taxonomy terms are taken.

lists.description: Flattened organization response for list endpoints. Excludes nested arrays.
Flattened service response for list endpoints. Excludes nested arrays.
Service at location response for list endpoints.
Taxonomy term results for list endpoints.

categories.infrastructure: Core definitions, patterns, and API specifications
categories.entities: Primary data entity schemas (organizations, services, locations, etc)
categories.lists: Flattened entity schemas for API list responses
